title: rubus koeleri
intro: Rubus koeleri, also known as the Koehler's black raspberry or thimbleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family. It is primarily found in North America and is known for its edible fruits, leaves, and roots, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant contains anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, potentially providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting cognitive function. Rubus koeleri can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also consumed fresh or dried as a fruit or leaf supplement.
